XGetLaunchInfo

The XGetLaunchInfo function retrieves the title's launch information.

DWORD XGetLaunchInfo(
  PDWORD  pdwLaunchDataType,
  PLAUNCH_DATA  pLaunchData
);

Parameters

pdwLaunchDataType
[out] Pointer to a DWORD variable that receives a value indicating the type of launch data contained in pLaunchData. The possible values are:
Value Meaning
LDT_FROM_DASHBOARD The title was launched by the Xbox Dashboard; an LD_FROM_DASHBOARD structure is copied to pLaunchData.
LDT_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE The title was launched by the remote debugger; an LD_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE structure is copied to pLaunchData.
LDT_FROM_UPDATE The title was launched by the Xbox Live updater after an update of the title; an LD_FROM_UPDATE structure is copied to pLaunchData. In this case, any data that existed on the utility drive (Drive Z:) before the update is deleted during the update process. (The utility drive is also known as the title cache.)
LDT_TITLE The title was launched using the XLaunchNewImage function; a LAUNCH_DATA structure is copied to pLaunchData.
pLaunchData
[out] Pointer to a LAUNCH_DATA structure that receives information concerning the title's launch. The contents returned in the structure depend on the value returned in the pdwLaunchDataType parameter. pLaunchData should be cast to the appropriate structure type (PLD_FROM_DASHBOARD, PLD_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE, or PLAUNCH_DATA) to access the launch information.

Return Values

If the function succeeds, the return value is ERROR_SUCCESS.

If the function fails, the return value is an error code defined in winerror.h. The function does not use SetLastError to set the calling thread's last-error code.

If no launch data was passed to the title, the function fails and returns ERROR_NOT_FOUND, without setting pdwLaunchDataType or filling in any launch data. Examples of this scenario are:

  • The title was launched by the debugger but no command-line arguments were specified.
  • The title was launched by turning on the Xbox console with the title disc already in the DVD drive.

Remarks

Use XGetLaunchInfo to determine the method used to launch the title and retrieve the associated launch information. The function fills in the LAUNCH_DATA structure pointed to by pLaunchData with information concerning the title's launch.

The value returned in the pdwLaunchDataType parameter indicates the method that was used to launch the title and also the type of data returned in pLaunchData.

If the launch type is LDT_FROM_DASHBOARD, the title was launched by the Xbox Dashboard; pLaunchData is filled in with an LD_FROM_DASHBOARD structure containing the dwContext value that was passed to the Dashboard when the title launched the Dashboard to perform some service. See XLaunchNewImage for more information.

If the launch type is LDT_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE, the title was launched by the remote debugger; pLaunchData is filled in with an LD_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE structure containing the command line passed to the title by the debugger.

If the launch type is LDT_FROM_UPDATE, the title was launched by the Xbox Live updater; pLaunchData is filled in with an LD_FROM_UPDATE structure.

If the launch type is LDT_TITLE, the title was launched by a call to XLaunchNewImage; pLaunchData is filled in with a LAUNCH_DATA structure containing the launch information specified in the call to XLaunchNewImage. A title can pass data only to itself, not another title. The title identifier of the title that called XLaunchNewImage must match the title identifier of the title calling XGetLaunchInfo.

Call XGetLaunchInfo only once per boot.

Command-line parameters can ba passed to an XBE from the XbReboot tool. The code example below demonstrates how to retrieve command-line parameters upon reboot.

DWORD dwLaunchDataType = 0;
LAUNCH_DATA LaunchData;

if( (ERROR_SUCCESS == XGetLaunchInfo(&dwLaunchDataType, &LaunchData) ) 
	&& (LDT_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE == dwLaunchDataType))
{
	char *szCmdLine = ((PLD_FROM_DEBUGGER_CMDLINE) (&LaunchData))->szCmdLine;
	//NOTE: szCmdLine is one string that contains the entire command line. 
	//you need to parse szCmdLine for each parameter
}
else
{
	//no command-line available 
}

Requirements

Header: Declared in xbox.h, for include use xtl.h.
Import Library: Use xapilib.lib

See Also

Executable File Functions | XLaunchNewImage
